prompt: This code is supposed to copy an array and double every element in the copy, but both arrays end up doubled. What is the bug?
choices: The for loop modifies copy but the *= operator does not work on arrays
int[] copy = original makes copy an alias, not a new array — both variables point to the same array
original must be declared final to prevent modification
System.out.println reads from copy instead of original
explanation: Arrays are objects; assignment copies the reference, not the data. copy and original point to the same array in memory. Fix: use Arrays.copyOf(original, original.length) or System.arraycopy to create a separate array.
